sections.content:

Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Green at rows 24-26, cols 20-23. White at rows 27-31, cols 20-23. Target at rows 32-35, cols 20-23 adjacent but not activated. Target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43 still uncollected. DOWN blocked by white.
- Goal model: Collect all three blue-square targets by moving adjacent and pressing SPACE.
- Action model: Movement moves player 4 cells. SPACE collects adjacent targets.
- Plan: Try RIGHT to move toward top-right target, then navigate to activate targets.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 72 actions; a strong score needs about 71 or fewer. You are 1.0x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: I see the green block is now to the right of the blue-gray bar. Let me check the current state to see if any targets were activated.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Player position
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
white = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"\nGreen: {green[0]['boundary'] if green else 'NOT FOUND'}")
print(f"White: {white[0]['boundary'] if white else 'NOT FOUND'}")

# Target frames
c_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 12]
print(f"\nTarget frames (c, 12px): {len(c_nodes)}")
for cn in c_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={cn['boundary']}")

# Check for G frames
G_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
print(f"G frames: {len(G_nodes)}")
for gn in G_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={gn['boundary']}")

# Blue centers
b_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 4]
print(f"Blue centers (4px): {len(b_nodes)}")
for bn in b_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={bn['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Green: rows 24-26, cols 40-43
- White: rows 27-31, cols 40-43
- Target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43 (above green)
- Target at rows 32-23, cols 20-23 (left of white)
- Blue centers: rows 21-22, cols 41-42 (inside top target), rows 29-30, cols 41-42 (inside white), rows 33-34, cols 21-22 (inside left target)
The green is now at rows 24-26, cols 40-43, which is directly below the target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43. The white is at rows 27-31, cols 40-43, below the green.
The target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43 is above the green. To activate it, I need to move UP so the white is between the green and the target. Let me try moving UP.
Wait, but the target is at rows 20-23, cols 40-43, and the green is at rows 24-26, cols 40-43. If I move UP, the green goes to rows 20-22, cols 40-43 (overlapping with the target). That might activate the target.
Actually, looking at the pattern from before:
- When green moved LEFT to rows 28-30, cols 16-19, the white was at row 31, cols 16-19 (below green), and the target at rows 32-35, cols 16-19 (below white) was activated.
- So the pattern is: green moves toward the target, white is between green and target, target activates.
In the current case:
- Green at rows 24-26, cols 40-43
- White at rows 27-31, cols 40-43 (below green)
- Target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43 (above green)
- White is BELOW green, not above. So the white is not between green and target.
If I move UP, the green goes to rows 20-22, cols 40-43, and the white would be at rows 23-26, cols 40-43 (below green). The target is at rows 20-23, cols 40-43, which overlaps with the green's new position. This might activate the target.
Let me try moving UP.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Green at rows 24-26, cols 40-43, below target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43. White at rows 27-31, cols 40-43 (below green). Target above green, white below green - white not between green and target.
Plan: Move UP to get green adjacent to/overlapping the target at rows 20-23, cols 40-43.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move UP to activate target above
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
